label: Day 8 — Afternoon: Farewell Networking & Legacy Wall
description: The final evening programme — a space for genuine connection, gratitude, and forward-looking energy. Not a formal ceremony but a human gathering: participants share what they are taking home, leave messages for each other, and celebrate what they built together.
objectives: Create space for genuine emotional closure and gratitude
Celebrate the collective work and outputs of the exchange
Build a lasting digital memory of the group
Strengthen individual connections across the four countries

steps.title: The giving circle
steps.body: Participants stand in a circle. Each person gives a genuine compliment or observation to ONE other person in the group. The rule: it must be specific, not generic.

steps.title: Legacy Wall
steps.body: Using the interactive wall below, each participant contributes: one memory from the week, one thing they learned, one green commitment they are taking home, and their contact. The wall is exported as a PDF and shared with all participants.

steps.title: Farewell dinner & free connection
steps.body: Dinner together, music, no structure. This is the most important part — don't programme it. Just protect the time and the space.

steps.title: Tomorrow's logistics
steps.body: Brief practical info about Day 9: timing, luggage, check-out, YouthPass session. Keep it short — don't kill the atmosphere.

facilitatorNote: The giving circle is one of the most powerful tools in a youth exchange — but only if you insist on specificity. The first few rounds will be generic; gently push back each time until the quality shifts.
